title: weinmannia bradfordii
intro: Weinmannia bradfordii, also known as the Tallowwood or Tawalla, is a species of flowering plant in the family Cunoniaceae. It is native to New Caledonia and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and its herbal preparations are used to treat a range of ailments, including colds, coughs, and skin conditions.
